qi.d: n. the circulating life energy that in Chinese philosophy is thought to be inherent in all things; in traditional Chinese medicine the balance of negative and positive forms in the body is believed to be essential for good health

qiana.t: n. 奎阿纳(一种蚕丝感较强、耐洗、不起皱的脂环族聚酰胺纤维)[商标名]

qiang.d: n. the Tibeto-Burman language spoken in Sichuan

qibla.t: 朝向（即麦加天房克尔白所在的方向, 为穆斯林朝拜方向）
qibla.d:
n. the direction of the Kaaba toward which Muslims turn for their daily prayers
n. a small terrorist group of Muslims in South Africa formed in the 1980s; was inspired by Ayatollah Khomeini to create an Islamic state in South Africa

qid.t: abbr. 一天四次quarter in die

qin.d: n. the Chinese dynasty (from 246 BC to 206 BC) that established the first centralized imperial government and built much of the Great Wall

qing.d: n. the last imperial dynasty of China (from 1644 to 1912) which was overthrown by revolutionaries; during the Qing dynasty China was ruled by the Manchu

qingdao.t: n. 青岛（中国山东省地名）

qinghai.t: 青海(位于中国西部, 长江、黄河上游)

qinhuangdao.t: 秦皇岛(市)[河北省] ( 旧译Ch'in- huang- tao 或Chinwangtao)

qintar.t: n. 昆塔(阿尔巴尼亚辅币名)
qintar.d: n 100 qindarka equal 1 lek in Albania

qio.t: [计]= Queue Input Output,队列输入输出

qipao.t: [网络] 女士旗袍；娆洪獥；旗袍裤

qiqihar.t: n. 齐齐哈尔（黑龙江省的地级市）

qis.t: [计]= Quality Insurance System,质量保证制度[系统]

qisam.t: [计] 排队索引顺序存取法

qishm.t: 格什姆岛(伊朗一岛, 在Hormuz海峡内)

qiviut.t: n. <美> 北极麝牛身上的绒毛
